---
title: "chipseeker_caras_zbtb20"
author: "G Sejourne"
date: "2025-10-15"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:



```{r}
# Install ChIPseeker and its dependencies if you don't have them
if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
    install.packages("BiocManager")
BiocManager::install("ChIPseeker")
BiocManager::install("TxDb.Mmusculus.UCSC.mm10.knownGene") # or another genome
```


```{r}
# Load the necessary libraries
library(ChIPseeker)
library(TxDb.Mmusculus.UCSC.mm10.knownGene)

# Load the annotation database
txdb <- TxDb.Mmusculus.UCSC.mm10.knownGene

# Run the annotation function on the output from CaRAS
# This will associate each peak with the nearest gene and its genomic location (e.g., promoter, intron).
annotated.peaks <- annotatePeak(
    './data/astro_zbtb20_all_peaks_calculated.narrowPeak', 
    tssRegion = c(-1500, 1500), 
    TxDb = txdb, 
    annoDb = "org.Mm.eg.db",
    verbose = FALSE
)

```

```{r}
# Convert the annotated peaks to a data frame
annotated.peaks.df <- as.data.frame(annotated.peaks)

# rename chipseeker annotations
names(annotated.peaks.df)[names(annotated.peaks.df) == "annotation"] <- "chipseeker_annot"

# read in the caraas annotations
caras_annotation <- read.delim('./data/astro_zbtb20_all_peaks_calculated.tsv')

# Merge chipseeker annotation column into caras_annotation dataframe
chipseeker_col <- annotated.peaks.df[, c("V4", "chipseeker_annot", "SYMBOL")]

merged_annotations <- merge(
  caras_annotation,
  chipseeker_col,
  by.x = "Peak.ID",
  by.y = "V4",
  all.x = TRUE
)

# View the results, which now include gene symbols
head(merged_annotations)

# Get a list of unique genes associated with differentially bound sites
gene.list <- unique(merged_annotations$Gene.Name)

# View the gene list
head(gene.list)

```

```{r}
library(tidyr)
# filter peaks to include only those called by at least 5 of 6 peakcallers
merged_annotations_filtered <- merged_annotations[merged_annotations$Peak.Caller.Overlaps>=5,]

# Split the 'chipseeker_annot' column by comma into 'annot_class', 'details'
merged_annotations_sep <- merged_annotations_filtered %>%
  separate(col = chipseeker_annot, into = c("annot_class", "details"), sep = " \\(")


# Write the list of genes to a file
write.csv(merged_annotations_sep, file="./data/caras_chipseeker_annotations_astro_zbtb20.csv", row.names=FALSE)

```

```{r}
# Get frequency table
freq_table <- table(merged_annotations_sep$annot_class)
print(freq_table)

# Get proportion table and multiply by 100 for percentages
percent_table <- prop.table(freq_table) * 100
print(percent_table)
```

```{r}
# genomic feature distribution plots
# install.packages("ggplot2")
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
#install.packages("geomtextpath")
library(geomtextpath)

# Convert frequency table to dataframe
zb_annot_table <- as.data.frame(freq_table)
colnames(zb_annot_table) <- c("Annot", "Frequency")
print(zb_annot_table)

# Calculate percentages
zb_annot_table <- zb_annot_table %>% 
  mutate(pct = paste(round(100 * Frequency / sum(Frequency), digits = 1), "%"))

# define legend labels
#legend_labels <- paste0(zb_annot_table$Annot, " (", zb_annot_table$pct, ")")
#names(legend_labels) <- zb_annot_table$Annot

my_colors = c(Promoter = "orange", Intron = "lightblue", 'Distal Intergenic' = "darkblue", Exon = "plum", "3' UTR" = "greenyellow", "5' UTR"  = "slateblue", Downstream = "mediumorchid")

pie <- ggplot(zb_annot_table, aes(x="", y=Frequency, fill=Annot)) +
  geom_bar(stat="identity", linewidth=0.5, color="black") +
  coord_polar("y", start=0) +
  
  theme_void() + # remove background, grid, numeric labels

  #geom_textpath(aes(label = Annot, x = 1.2), color = "black", fontface = "bold", size = 6, position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), hjust = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_colors,
                    labels = zb_annot_table$Annot,
                    name = "Annotation") +
  ggtitle("Zbtb20 Astro annotations") +
  theme(
      legend.title = element_text(size = 24), # Changes legend title size
      legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
      #legend.position = "bottom"
  )

  ggsave(
    filename = "zbtb20_annot_pie_astro.png",  # or .pdf, .tiff, etc.
    plot = pie,
    #width = 12,    # width in inches
    #height = 12,   # height in inches
    #dpi = 300     # resolution (good for publication)
  )
pie
```
